key_finding: H2O heavy-hitters do NOT overlap with answer-coupled positions (rho=0.004-0.11). H2O compression preferentially retains TC-selective (57%) over AC-selective (40%) positions. Standard KV compression may actively harm the hidden channel. Also: Qwen3-4B uses GQA (8 KV heads), invalidating GQA-vs-MHA hypothesis.
evidence_impact: Natural usage gap partially addressed — but in the NEGATIVE direction. H2O does not implicitly preserve the hidden channel. New practical implication: AC-aware compression could outperform standard methods. GQA/MHA hypothesis invalidated.
next_planned: AC-aware compression ablation test (retain AC positions vs H2O positions and measure accuracy difference) or investigate what actually explains Qwen/Llama encoding differences if not GQA

spatial_structure.status: strong — now shown to be distinct from H2O importance

natural_usage.status: negative — H2O does NOT preserve hidden channel

literature_grounding.status: strong — GQA hypothesis invalidated by discovery
